programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The lawman begins making real progress in ending The Chairman's reign of terror. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: Contraband (2012)
programme.desc: Successful international smuggler Chris Farraday turns his back on a life of crime to raise a family. However, his brother-in-law crosses a powerful drug lord, who forces Chris to transport billions of dollars of counterfeit money into the US, or his wife and children will be killed. Crime thriller, starring Mark Wahlberg, Kate Beckinsale, Giovanni Ribisi and Ben Foster

programme.title: Robin Hood (2018)
programme.desc: Battle-hardened Crusader Robin of Loxley and his Moorish commander launch an audacious rebellion against the corrupt English crown. However, they face tough competition from the twisted Sheriff of Nottingham. Director Otto Bathurst, who also worked on Peaky Blinders and His Dark Materials, delivers his take on the classic adventure yarn. Taron Egerton plays the heroic outlaw, with Jamie Foxx, Ben Mendelsohn and Tim Minchin also starring

programme.title: Warrior (2011)
programme.desc: Two estranged brothers are drawn to enter a mixed martial arts tournament, one a military veteran looking for a way to escape his troubled past, the other a struggling teacher who needs the prize money to support his family. As they progress through the contest, both emerge as likely winners - putting them on course to face each other in the final. Drama, with Joel Edgerton, Tom Hardy and Nick Nolte

programme.title: Sword of Vengeance (2015)
programme.desc: In 11th century Britain, a mysterious stranger known only as Shadow Walker returns home after years in slavery and immediately turns a group of local men into ruthless killers - but do they have the ability to overthrow their tyrannical ruler? Action adventure, with Stanley Weber and Annabelle Wallis

programme.title: Seal Team VI: Journey into Darkness (2008)
programme.desc: A black-ops team find their covert mission in Iraq compromised, and the harrowing consequences change the course of the conflict. Drama based on real events, starring Ken Gamble

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Chaos reigns in a hotel as one person is accused of assaulting another, and the police are called in while paramedics try to treat both parties at the same time. A trip over a pet dog leaves a woman with a wound on her arm that won't stop bleeding. Plus, a 76-year-old woman with fluctuating blood pressure confides in the paramedics that she's grieving the loss of her husband

programme.title: Our Tiny Islands
programme.desc: A guide to self-sufficient island life, from off-grid living on the stunning Scottish island of Gometra to a special branch of Hare Krishnas on Lough Erne in Northern Ireland. Meanwhile, on Sark, island life is the perfect fit for a young family who are building their own home, beekeeping and serving as their community's first responders

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras reveal how individuals adapt and survive in the face of adversity. Subjects include Paul, 60, who is rushed to A&E after colliding with a van. Doctors are concerned that he may have serious injuries to his pelvis and spine. There's also a profile of four-year-old Jeremy, who is struggling to breathe. He suffers from a series of chronic conditions, including microcephaly and epilepsy

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow patients at south-west London's St George's hospital on Harry and Meghan's wedding day in May 2018. As the happy couple tie the knot, patients and their relatives reflect on the meaning of lifelong love, devotion and commitment. Among those treated are David, 74, who is flown to St George's by air ambulance after falling from a ladder. Medics suspect internal bleeding and he is rushed to CT to assess the damage. His wife Lynda makes her way to the hospital as doctors try to prevent catastrophic blood loss

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: Midway through their North Yorkshire tour the campers visit Masons Campsite near Skipton for an exhilarating stay involving river swimming, trout fishing, cricket and croissants

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit a Yorkshire textile mill, where Simon repurposes a mill trolley into a glass-top coffee table, and Henry struggles to repair a Soviet-era motorbike

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole head to Surrey and Hampshire. Simon builds a huge kitchen table from discarded rafters and gives a 1970s campervan a new lease of life. Henry hankers after an American tractor and Royal Enfield motorbike and sidecar

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to Inn at the Park in Aberdeen, where for owners Ronnie and Steph Caird, it's all about keeping it in the family. When the guests arrive, design-conscious father and son Steve and Allard take a trip back in time, and John isn't flushed with praise for his bathroom. At dinner, John reveals his culinary background, piling on the pressure for Ronnie to deliver a good breakfast. Fiona and Shauna's sizeable confession raises eyebrows, while in the morning, Ronnie has a breakfast to forget and the bathroom blues leave Ronnie and Steph seeking answers from their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Ingliston Country Club in Renfrewshire, where events manager Shauna Kirk and sales manager Fiona Gallagher prove a force to be reckoned with. Guests Steve and Allard aren't feeling the dated décor, and John proves that one size doesn't fit all. Ronnie and Steph are blown away by the competition, but they think that perfection comes at a price

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: For the third visit, the group heads to 1692 Wasdale in the Lake District, where owners Steve and Allard Newell have proudly built their B&B from the ground up

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests stay at the Lynbar Hotel in Blackpool, where Ronnie and Steph have parking problems, while confident owner John O'Donnell boasts of serving the best breakfast in town

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to settle some scores. Inn at the Park owners Ronnie and Steph Caird are intent on clearing up John's issues with the toilet

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Wrestling promoter Dale Harvey hosts the first dinner party in Derby, where he hopes to impress aspiring thespian Beth Dunmore, business support manager Jo-Anna, mother-of-three Ondine Shardlow, and triathlete David Morris with a wrestling-themed extravaganza featuring large men in tight lycra. However, a fractious royal debate dampens the mood

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Business support manager Jo-Anna hosts the second dinner party in Derby, and hopes to wow her guests with a glitz and glam evening - complete with ball gowns and poetry. However, David is hoping for a more lively night - and his antics do not impress fellow contestant Dale

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: PR whizz Beth Dunmore hosts the third night in Derby, and she hopes to impress her guests with her vaguely Middle Eastern menu. However, she has her work cut out when it comes to the fussy David Morris, whose confidence seems to be growing as the week progresses. Beth has a fan in cruise ship connoisseur Jo-Anna, though, while single mum Ondine Shardlow is disappointed with the level of competition and thinks she has the week in the bag

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Ondine Shardlow hosts the fourth dinner party in Derby, hoping to impress her guests with a flavoursome Greek holiday-inspired menu. However, her carefully planned evening takes a turn for the worse when she manages to insult Dale, and Jo-Anna displays some questionable table manners

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the final day of the dinner party contest in Derby and the turn of triathlete David Morris to serve his offerings. Ondine is not impressed by his small portions, big flavours approach, and is even less satisfied with Jo-Anna's behaviour during the week. The host throws an impromptu work-out class where wrestling promoter Dale Harvey is put through his paces, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Farmer's daughter Mandy Sowray and her friend Anny Harrison host first at Laskill Country House in Helmsley, North Yorkshire. Chris Witton and Saroop Ram from the Swan Hotel feel right at home, but tech-savvy Colin and Ben Warburg from Scalford Hall Hotel fail to connect from the start. During the afternoon's hay-bale race, youngsters Charlie Thompson and Lauren Tucker from the Abbey Hotel fail to stack up to the challenge. At dinner, Mandy bigs up her banger qualifications, but at breakfast she fails to crack Chris's order. Colin's feedback leaves unanswered questions for Mandy

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Chris Witton and his spiritual friend Rammy hope that straightforward value for money will win them the competition, as they host at the Swan Hotel in Telford. A Yorkshire pudding-making competition leaves confident cooks Mandy and Anny thinking there's more to youngsters Charlie and Lauren than meets the eye. At dinner, Rammy opens up about his troubled past. Colin has a rude awakening in the morning. At feedback time, Chris has a change of heart about some of his guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Colin Warburg and his student son Ben hope that their 81-room country pile - Scalford Hall Hotel in Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire - will wow their rivals. However, Chris and Rammy are still smarting from their feedback and think that Colin is playing games. After an afternoon crate stacking competition on the hotel's high wire course, Colin gets a grilling at dinner. A lukewarm reaction to breakfast only adds to Colin's woes and when he sees his feedback he suspects that retribution is at hand

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to the Abbey Hotel in Battle, East Sussex, run by confident young colleagues Charlie Thompson and Lauren Tucker. However when Charlie and Lauren's guests arrive, Colin and Ben feel the squeeze and Chris and Rammy find themselves in a sticky situation. On an afternoon at the archery range, scores are settled. At dinner, straight-talking Chris can't help but be impressed, but when the conversation turns to parking, the guests are in a jam with their hosts. Later, Colin's feedback leaves Lauren wanting answers

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet up to settle some scores. Laskill Country House host Mandy wants to crack where she went wrong with breakfast and why Colin and Ben failed to connect with her, while Chris and Rammy from The Swan Hotel wish to question Lauren on her room flaws and get to the bottom of Colin's bedroom nightmares

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp meets Stuart and Lara, who want to be under the same roof after two years of being together, but living 100 miles apart. The pair look in the vicinity of Amersham, while Phil Spencer meets Randhir and Sheetal. They want to swap London living for village life in Berkshire before baby number two arrives in five weeks

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts the seventh heat, as five more players are put through their paces and the toughest music questions on TV

programme.title: Ancient Greece by Train with Alice Roberts
programme.desc: Alice is in the Penteli hills in Athens, at a site known as the Sanctuary of Dionysus, the god of wine. Local legend has it that on a night of drink and revelry a man called Thespis created what we now know as theatre. In a surprise invitation to meet the Greek Minister of Culture, Alice discusses the return of the Elgin Marbles

programme.title: Castle Secrets
programme.sub-title: Gateway to Hell; Monk's Curse; The Queen and the Concubine
programme.desc: A look at Houska Castle, a gothic chapel erected over a supposed gateway to hell and how a property dispute between an eminent family became the catalyst for tragedy. Plus, exploring how a renaissance chateau provided an opulent backdrop for a bitter rivalry between powerful women

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This edition features stories of human compassion and kindness in the most challenging circumstances. Allen, 82, has injured his chest after a dizzy spell caused him to fall down 13 steps. As son Graham waits nervously for doctors to assess Allen's injuries, Graham describes his dad's strength and resilience in carrying the family through dark times, and how precious the bond between father and son can be

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow Aydigul, 38, who has suspected sepsis following major abdominal surgery. Her sister is at her bedside throughout, and discusses their childhood in Tajikistan and her extraordinary strength in the face of adversity. Plus, emergency consultant Ahmed rushes to save the life of a woman who has an abdominal aortic aneurysm

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Forty-year-old Tania is rushed to St George's with life-threatening injuries, having been knocked over while crossing a road, while semi-professional footballer Lilli arrives in A&E after being elbowed in the face during a match. Seventy-seven-year-old Edwin is rushed to resus bleeding from a tumour on his neck, and as the medical team examines the wound, his partner Jeanette explains how their romance blossomed in later life. When a maxillofacial specialist arrives to plan the next stage of Edwin's treatment, it forces his family to confront the very painful truth

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Chaos reigns in a hotel as one person is accused of assaulting another, and the police are called in while paramedics try to treat both parties at the same time. A trip over a pet dog leaves a woman with a wound on her arm that won't stop bleeding. Plus, a 76-year-old woman with fluctuating blood pressure confides in the paramedics that she's grieving the loss of her husband

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: Camping holidaymakers Sugapuff and Therryi, Nik and Harriet, and Emmy and Simon pitch up at a working farm on the edge of Ilkley Moor, West Yorkshire. Shearing sheep and feeding lambs are among the new experiences for this crowd of campsite critics

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The programme heads to Lowestoft in Suffolk, where Henry Cole picks a valuable Lyons Tea enamel sign while Simon O'Brien converts a station trolley into a garden sofa

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The duo rummage through the hangar of a Vulcan bomber, where Henry finds a cockpit seat to restore, and Simon attempts to turn part of a jet engine into a coffee table

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts in Pencader, Carmarthenshire, at the School House B&B, owned by eco-friendly married couple Jayne and Ian Hall-Edwards, who hope that their environmental approach will impress their guests. At dinner, Ian reveals that his hosting skills might not be up to scratch and eyebrows are raised at straight-talking colleagues Jo and Neil. At breakfast, assumptions are made of businessman Raj

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Cathedral 73 in Cardiff, managed by Neil Chidzey. On arrival, guests are wowed by the glamorous rooms, but Raj is shocked to find breakfast is not included

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at Leafy Suburban B&B in Northwood, Hertfordshire, where the guests spend the afternoon taking a swing at some golfing

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The last visit is to the Wellington Inn, Hertfordshire, where Felicity Rainbow takes a spiritual approach, cleansing the aura of the rooms and encouraging guests to feel the vibe

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. The School House B&B is first and host Jayne Hall-Edwards takes guests back to basics over her green policy. Next, Neil Chidzey and Jo Goodwin from Cathedral 73 challenge their guests over costings

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The competition heads to Manchester, where battling it out for the £1,000 cash prize are part-time DJ Dom, 35-year old barber Danny, trainee barrister Sasha, nurse Katherine and goth Maria. In the first edition, DJ Dom kicks off the week serving up a menu inspired by the 1990s 'Madchester' music scene

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Trainee barrister Sasha hosts the second night in Manchester, where she hopes to impress her guests with a Chinese-Pakistani fusion menu. Meanwhile, fellow contestant Maria reveals more about her love of gothic culture, and Danny discusses his soon-to-be-written autobiography

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nurse Katherine hosts the third dinner party in Manchester, and she opts for a simple menu of a smoked salmon with horseradish cream starter, and a chorizo and chickpea main course. At the table, direct questioning and some extraordinary revelations cause some of the guests to feel uncomfortable

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Barber Danny hosts the fourth night of the competition in Manchester, and decides on an evening of Bulgarian cuisine as a homage to his wife. His party ends with some Bulgarian language lessons that bemuse fellow diners Dom and Maria

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the final night in Manchester and glamorous goth Maria is hoping to wow her guests with her twist on Asian cuisine. Some of the guests are feeling a little uneasy that the host will impart her love of vampire culture upon her menu, but the blood-free dishes even manage to impress daddy's girl Sasha. However, an avant-garde transgender dancer creates a stir, and at one point it seems possible that Maria has bitten off more than she can chew

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras reveal how individuals adapt and survive in the face of adversity. Subjects include Paul, 60, who is rushed to A&E after colliding with a van. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Ondine Shardlow hosts the fourth dinner party in Derby, hoping to impress her guests with a flavoursome Greek holiday-inspired menu. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Four in a Bed

Chris Witton and Saroop Ram from the Swan Hotel feel right at home, but tech-savvy Colin and Ben Warburg from Scalford Hall Hotel fail to connect from the start. During the afternoon's hay-bale race, youngsters Charlie Thompson and Lauren Tucker from the Abbey Hotel fail to stack up to the challenge. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: The World After Stonehenge
programme.desc: Neil Oliver explores the origins of ancient Britain and its people. He dives for 3,000-year-old treasure and potholes through an ancient copper mine to discover how a golden age of bronze gave way to a period of social and economic crisis and sharp climate change, eventually to be replaced by a new era of iron

programme.title: WWI: The Last Tommies
programme.desc: Veterans of the First World War recall their experiences, with archive footage used to illustrate events, beginning with the first two years of the war in 1914 and '15. The men - aged 90 and more when interviewed - reveal how they survived and kept their spirits up in the trenches with death all around them, while there is a look at how families at home faced up to the absence and loss of husbands, fathers, sweethearts and son

Grant Williams returns to Derry to spend more time with his daughter Wendy O'Hagan to make the most of their time together in light of his recent diagnosis of Alzheimer's, while Colin Deering - who first met his brother Bryan back in 2014 - starts out on a new journey to find out about his birth father

programme.title: Long Lost Family

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Retired heptathlon star Jessica Ennis-Hill sets out to discover the truth behind a family mystery concerning her great-great-grandmother. When his father, Jess's great-grandfather was a child his mother Emily Maud, known as Maud, walked out. The family never talked about Maud, and Jess's grandfather has no idea what became of her. Jess wants to investigate what could have happened to make her two-time great-grandmother abandon her children and effectively be written out of the family's history. She also examines her Jamaican four-time great-grandfather's journey from enslavement to land ownership

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Ralf Little heads to the Orkney Islands off the north coast of Scotland to investigate his grandfather Arthur's experience during the Second World War. He is astonished to discover that Arthur was caught up in one of the most important battles of the war. On another branch of the family tree, Ralf is thrilled to confirm a family rumour that he is linked to a Welsh international footballer - his great grandfather. On returning to his home town of Manchester, the actor is delighted to learn that his four-times great grandfather played a key role in the industrial development of the city

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell investigate the case of Maria Roberts, the daughter of Italian parents who had a happy adoption growing up with an English family, and now hopes to find Gilda, her birth mother. The team also meets Francesca Barnes, who is in the early stages of transitioning from male to female. She was born with the name Paul and given up for adoption as a baby. Francesca is now searching for her mother, Norma, but fears her transition may prove too much of a challenge, and lead to rejection

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Growing up, Barbara Jacobs always believed that she was one of three children. It was not until she reached her twenties that she made a discovery which would change her life. Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell investigate Barbara's story, and also Sharon Hearte's. She was mixed race child adopted by a white family who grew up in the West Midlands. Sharon knew her birth mother, Barbara, was a white woman who worked in a Smethwick bicycle factory, and her father was from Jamaica. She wants to track down her mother to tell her that she understands why she was given up for adoption

Mark Ratcliffe grew up in Lancashire and at the age of 12 his parents told him he was adopted and the revelation devastated him. programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell revisit the stories of three people navigating new relationships and complicated feelings, including ex-footballer Dominic Matteo, who was desperate to find his birth mother after being diagnosed with a brain tumour. Grant Williams returns to Derry to spend more time with his daughter Wendy O'Hagan to make the most of their time together in light of his recent diagnosis of Alzheimer's, while Colin Deering - who first met his brother Bryan back in 2014 - starts out on a new journey to find out about his birth father

programme.title: Long Lost Family

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Retired heptathlon star Jessica Ennis-Hill sets out to discover the truth behind a family mystery concerning her great-great-grandmother. When his father, Jess's great-grandfather was a child his mother Emily Maud, known as Maud, walked out. The family never talked about Maud, and Jess's grandfather has no idea what became of her. Jess wants to investigate what could have happened to make her two-time great-grandmother abandon her children and effectively be written out of the family's history. She also examines her Jamaican four-time great-grandfather's journey from enslavement to land ownership

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Ralf Little heads to the Orkney Islands off the north coast of Scotland to investigate his grandfather Arthur's experience during the Second World War. He is astonished to discover that Arthur was caught up in one of the most important battles of the war. On another branch of the family tree, Ralf is thrilled to confirm a family rumour that he is linked to a Welsh international footballer - his great grandfather. On returning to his home town of Manchester, the actor is delighted to learn that his four-times great grandfather played a key role in the industrial development of the city

programme.title: Long Lost Family

Sharon knew her birth mother, Barbara, was a white woman who worked in a Smethwick bicycle factory, and her father was from Jamaica. She wants to track down her mother to tell her that she understands why she was given up for adoption

programme.title: Maigret

programme.title: Swindlers
programme.desc: The story of Dublin solicitor Thomas Byrne, who stole ?52 million from the banks and took ownership of 12 of his victims' houses in one of the biggest cases of white-collar crime in the history of the Irish State. This documentary charts how the solicitor scammed his neighbours and clients by transferring a dozen properties into his own name and lending from multiple banks using the houses as collateral

programme.title: Daniel O'Connell: The Emancipator
programme.desc: The year 2025 marked the 250th anniversary of the birth of Daniel O'Connell, the Kerry barrister and MP who became one of Ireland's greatest figures by securing Catholic emancipation, pioneering non-violent mass political mobilisation, and inspiring democratic movements around the world. This documentary tells his story, with insights by leading historians and scholars, including former President Mary McAleese, Declan Kiberd, Jay Rosman, Patrick Geoghegan, Niamh Gallagher and Chanté Mouton-Kinyon. Narrated by Domhnall Gleeson

programme.title: Welcome To Moore Street
programme.desc: Cameras follow Polish gym owner Pawel as he and his daughter chase her MMA dreams, and street photographer Aarif as he documents rising tensions in the city centre. programme.title: Kerry: Tides of Time
programme.desc: Part one of two. The story of the county's landscape, from its earliest origins, deep in the geological past, right up to the present day, told by those who know it intimately. programme.title: The Michelangelo Code: Secrets of the Sistine Chapel
programme.desc: Art critic Waldemar Januszczak searches for the code hidden in the ceiling of the chapel of the Apostolic Palace in Vatican City, overturning the myths surrounding the masterpiece

programme.title: Queen: Greatest Video Hits 1
programme.desc: A compilation of the band's promos from 1973 to 1981, featuring the videos for Bohemian Rhapsody, You're My Best Friend, Killer Queen, Bicycle Race and more

programme.title: The Seventies
programme.sub-title: The State of the Union Is Not Good
programme.desc: The documentary examines how America went from bad to worse as it dealt with one crisis after another throughout the presidential terms of Gerald Ford and Jimmy Carter

programme.title: Rolling Stone: Stories from the Edge
programme.desc: A look at the rise of Trump's America, Jann Wenner's 2016 post-election interview with Barack Obama, and the magazine's relationship with Bob Dylan

programme.title: Auction
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of a sale of the Queen frontman's personal effects at Sotheby's in London, telling his story through the items up for auction. Spanning art, fashion, musical instruments, personal papers and possessions, candid photographs, furniture, and jewels, accrued over the course of 50 years, the sales allowed the public to bid for a piece of rock 'n' roll history

programme.title: Painting Birds with Jim and Nancy Moir
programme.desc: The couple are joined by pianist and broadcaster Jools Holland and local artist David Hunt as they travel to Kent to paint the long-eared owl

programme.title: Mozart's Women: A Musical Journey
programme.desc: Lauren Laverne presents as the ENO Orchestra and opera soloists go on musical journey through the life of Mozart, putting the spotlight on the relationships with his real-life leading ladies. With arias from Così fan tutte, The Magic Flute amd Don Giovanni

programme.title: Atomic
programme.desc: Max and JJ try to win back the uranium in Casablanca, but the pair face a choice as the cartel closes in. Action adventure, with Alfie Allen and Shazad Latif

programme.title: Task
programme.sub-title: Out Beyond Ideas of Wrongdoing and Rightdoing, There Is a River
programme.desc: The task force, Dark Hearts and Robbie converge in the woods, leading to a dramatic showdown with far-reaching consequences for all involved. In the aftermath, Tom turns his attention back to his now fragmented team as the FBI launches an investigation

programme.title: Task
programme.desc: In the wake of the showdown, the members of the now-disbanded Task Force continue to seek answers. As the Dark Hearts mobilise to cover their tracks, a shocking revelation leads them to a final confrontation

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: The body of a girl is found with a broken rib which punctured her lung and 26 belt buckle marks on her body. A social worker confirms abuse suspicions, leading Tim and Frank to question the mother and her live-in boyfriend. Kellerman is exonerated by the Grand Jury but still feels tainted by suspicion. Bayliss reveals his own childhood abuse to Pembleton.

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming examines the hidden histories of cities, revealing the sights and sounds rarely witnessed by the public. He begins in London at St Paul's Cathedral, where he visits a place tourists never go, before exploring a hidden park and a little-known church. He also gets a guided tour of Soho from broadcaster Danny Baker

programme.title: Sort Of
programme.desc: BRLiN prepares for an important installation, Deenzie takes Sabi on a field trip and Paul joins a support group, finding comfort in a friend

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming tours the less-celebrated sights of Brighton, beginning by visiting the Toy and Model Museum to look at its collection of model trains. He then discovers a boutique that sells a variety of unusual items, before taking a glimpse behind the scenes at the Royal Pavilion

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Maritime Inn landlady Pat gets her first full house of the season as Brixham's tourist trade picks up for the summer - but refuses to hire anyone to help her with the extra work. Powerboat instructor Mike Weatherbee tries to enforce the laws of the sea, carpet-cleaner Dave Satchwill prepares to lead the town's popular ghost tour, and lone fisherman Dave Brown reveals his unusual methods

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: August arrives and the fishermen of Brixham spend time ashore with their families as their boats are repaired - but summer proves costly for Jan, who loses £1,000 for every day his vessel is out of action. Apprentice deckhand Logan has to decide whether a life at sea is the right choice for him and his family, and seafood seller Jenny receives a surprise on her 65th birthday

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: The body of a girl is found with a broken rib which punctured her lung and 26 belt buckle marks on her body. A social worker confirms abuse suspicions, leading Tim and Frank to question the mother and her live-in boyfriend. Kellerman is exonerated by the Grand Jury but still feels tainted by suspicion. Bayliss reveals his own childhood abuse to Pembleton.

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: The team investigate the killing of an Asian shopkeeper who tried to forbid dope dealers from operating outside his store. His killing leads detectives to Mahoney, whose parting shot to Kellerman throws the detective into a tailspin of depression

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: Following the opening of the bank, Alma charms Merrick into endorsing her liquidity, and it isn't long before she reverts to her old ways. Swearengen tries to figure out Hearst's intentions as he finally finds a use for Farnum. Meanwhile, two more actors arrive in town to be reunited with Langrishe as the troupe prepares to transform Chez Ami into a theatre. Ian McShane stars

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: Bullock and Swearengen contemplate a pre-emptive strike against Hearst, whose anger has been inflamed of late. However, the ruthless mining baron is preoccupied with Alma, whose bad habits lose her the bank services of Trixie, and possibly the affections of husband Ellsworth. Meanwhile, Aunt Lou Marchbank's long lost son arrives in town, but is soon sent packing. Starring Ian McShane

programme.title: Billions
programme.desc: As the Commission's announcement draws near, Prince must decide how far he is willing to go to secure his bid. Mase Carb develops a new algorithm

programme.title: Billions
programme.desc: Prince encourages his team to find new investments as Wendy prepares for the future. Rian comes to an unlikely arrangement with Wags

programme.title: My Brilliant Friend
programme.desc: Suspicions fall on Solaras when a child disappears from the neighbourhood, while Lila becomes consumed by her own conspiracies. In Italian

programme.title: Succession
programme.desc: While Logan doles out an unsavoury task to Roman ahead of his trip to meet Matsson, Connor fixates on small details at his wedding.

programme.desc: As the Commission's announcement draws near, Prince must decide how far he is willing to go to secure his bid. Mase Carb develops a new algorithm

programme.title: Billions
programme.desc: Prince encourages his team to find new investments as Wendy prepares for the future. Rian comes to an unlikely arrangement with Wags

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Rhaenyra questions Daemon's loyalty as Otto schemes to turn the public against her, while Ser Criston Cole concocts a misguided plan for revenge

programme.title: Prisoner
programme.desc: Amber reunites with her family while Tibor faces his estranged mother, while Olly rejects their plan and contacts the National Crime Unit, leading Pegasus straight to them

programme.title: Ponies
programme.desc: In 1977, two secretaries working at the US embassy in Moscow volunteer to be operatives after their CIA agent husbands die under mysterious circumstances. Thrown together by grief and circumstance, Bea Grant and Twila Hasbec dig into the vast conspiracy their husbands were killed for. Spy thriller, starring Emilia Clarke and Haley Lu Richardson.

programme.title: Succession
programme.desc: Shiv weighs a proposal from Matsson ahead of Waystar's Investor Day, while Kendall and Roman try to match Logan's leadership style

programme.title: Succession
programme.desc: The Waystar team heads to Norway to meet Matsson and his staff, while Shiv, Kendall and Roman consider whether to fight for Logan's original deal

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: As Rhaenyra struggles to come to terms with her son's murder, Alicent grows concerned by Aegon's Small Council. Return of the fantasy drama set 200 years before the events of Game of Thrones and telling the story of House Targaryen. Starring Matt Smith and Emma D'Arcy

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Rhaenyra questions Daemon's loyalty as Otto schemes to turn the public against her, while Ser Criston Cole concocts a misguided plan for revenge

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: The team investigate the killing of an Asian shopkeeper who tried to forbid dope dealers from operating outside his store. His killing leads detectives to Mahoney, whose parting shot to Kellerman throws the detective into a tailspin of depression

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Pembleton and Lewis are assigned to the murder of a wealthy woman. Each focuses on a different suspect - the victim's brother and an angry, talented art student from the poor side of town. A diamond ring with great sentimental value disappeared from the woman's finger after the body was found, and proves pivotal to the case

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming explores the history of Bristol, beginning by learning a grisly secret at the docks before meeting Mercury Prize-winning musician Roni Size to discuss life in the St Pauls and Stokes Croft areas of the city. He also explores the district of Clifton and visits a speakeasy-style bar

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor returns to explore more of the world's most luxurious hotels and begins in Miami's South Beach, home to some of the most hedonistic and extravagant establishments in America. At the Fontainebleau, he meets a man with eye-opening stories about Frank Sinatra's regular visits and gets a shock when he discovers how much it costs to hire a booth in the night club. Richard also gatecrashes swimwear fashion week at the Raleigh Hotel, where he speaks to the event manager about the many celebrity parties he has organised

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: In this edition the actor explores the luxury hotels of Hong Kong. Home to more skyscrapers than any other city, he first checks in to the Mandarin in the financial district, before experiencing the Four Seasons, the only hotel with two Michelin three-star resturants, as well as the timeless Peninsula in Kowloon, which even provides a Rolls-Royce airport taxi service. He ends his stay at the Ritz-Carlton, home to the world's highest swimming pool

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Pembleton and Lewis are assigned to the murder of a wealthy woman. Each focuses on a different suspect - the victim's brother and an angry, talented art student from the poor side of town. A diamond ring with great sentimental value disappeared from the woman's finger after the body was found, and proves pivotal to the case

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: When a police officer is found shot dead, Elizabeth Wu writes an article praising his dedication to the job, only for an informer to call her with a contradiction - the cop was targeted when he demanded his money back in a dope deal. Meanwhile, Kellerman's rogue brothers show up and he winds up in a jail cell with them

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: Blazanov commits a violation for the good of the camp, and the elders hold a meeting, intending to form a plan to deal with ruthless gold baron Hearst. Meanwhile, the renovations at Langrishe's theatre are delayed. Drama, with Ian McShane, Brian Cox and Pavel Lychnikoff

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: Bullock's letter is published in The Pioneer as the camp put their plan to deal with the villainous Hearst into action. Meanwhile, Wyatt Earp and his brother arrive in town as heroes after saving a sabotaged stagecoach - but there could be more to the newcomers than meets the eye. Drama, guest starring Gale Harold, with Timothy Olyphant

programme.desc: Chuck fights to unlock the city for the people. Meanwhile, Prince and his brains trust hatch a plan to turn the tables, and Taylor teams up with Philip

programme.sub-title: The Red Dragon and the Gold

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor explores Berlin, visiting the arty Propeller Island City Lodge - a hotel where guests can fulfil their fantasies by sleeping in lion cages, coffins or rotating beds. At the Schlosshotel in affluent Grunewald, cabaret dancer Romy Haag talks to Richard about her affair with David Bowie, who stayed at the hotel while living in the German capital in the 1970s

programme.title: The Mechanic (2011)
programme.desc: A professional hitman lets his emotional detachment slip when his mentor is murdered, and puts his deadly skills to the task of getting revenge. The solitary assassin finds an ally in his teacher's son, who also yearns for vengeance and persuades the killer to teach him the tricks of the trade. Action thriller remake, starring Jason Statham, Ben Foster and Donald Sutherland.

programme.title: Serenity (2019)
programme.desc: Baker Dill lives a peaceful life as a fishing boat captain, leading tours off a tranquil cove. His peace is shattered when estranged ex-wife Karen reappears, begging for him to help save her and their son from her violent husband. Thriller, written and directed by Peaky Blinders creator Steven Knight, starring Matthew McConaughey, Anne Hathaway and Diane Lane

programme.title: Sky Cinema Preview - May 2026
programme.desc: A look ahead at the films to enjoy this month on Sky Cinema

programme.title: Eye in the Sky (2015)
programme.desc: Colonel Katherine Powell's mission to wipe out a terrorist cell in Kenya is complicated when a girl enters the blast radius of the intended drone strike. Powell has to decide between sacrificing the girl's life or letting the terrorists flee to carry out more atrocities. Thriller, starring Helen Mirren, Alan Rickman and Aaron Paul

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows (2011)
programme.desc: The detective investigates a series of seemingly unconnected crimes all around the world, and becomes convinced one man is behind them all - the elusive Professor Moriarty. He sets forth on an international journey to bring the evil mastermind to justice, but finds he is dealing with a foe who is his intellectual equal. Action thriller sequel, starring Robert Downey Jr, Jude Law, Noomi Rapace and Jared Harris

programme.title: Nightcrawler (2014)
programme.desc: A cynical petty crook is inspired when he sees a freelance camera crew, and decides to go into business filming crime scenes and accidents, then selling the footage to news broadcasters. His determination to stay ahead of the competition leads him down a dark path as his methods become increasingly ruthless. Crime drama, starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Rene Russo and Bill Paxton

programme.title: The Mechanic (2011)
programme.desc: A professional hitman lets his emotional detachment slip when his mentor is murdered, and puts his deadly skills to the task of getting revenge. The solitary assassin finds an ally in his teacher's son, who also yearns for vengeance and persuades the killer to teach him the tricks of the trade. Action thriller remake, starring Jason Statham, Ben Foster and Donald Sutherland.

programme.title: Ocean's 8 (2018)
programme.desc: Following in the footsteps of brother Danny, light-fingered Debbie Ocean plans to steal the Toussaint, a $150million necklace from Vogue's Met Gala, with the 'help' of a famous actress. Crime caper, starring Sandra Bullock, Cate Blanchett, Anne Hathaway and Mindy Kaling

programme.title: Captain Phillips (2013)
programme.desc: An American cargo ship sets a dangerous course around the coast of Somalia, while inland, four men are pressed into service as pirates by the local warlords. The captain is taken hostage when the raiding party hijacks the vessel, resulting in a tense five-day crisis. Fact-based thriller, starring Tom Hanks and Barkhad Abdi

programme.title: The Bourne Legacy (2012)
programme.desc: The leaders of a secret government agency plan to eliminate several agents as part of a cover-up when they face having their dark secrets exposed. One of the targets is able to evade his fate and go on the run - but his escape is threatened by his dependence on drugs to boost his physical and mental capabilities. Spy thriller sequel, with Jeremy Renner, Rachel Weisz and Edward Norton

programme.title: Inception (2010)
programme.desc: A talented thief uses hi-tech devices to enter other people's dreams so he can steal their secrets. An industrialist hires him to perform a far more challenging job - to implant an idea into a corporate heir's mind, so he will think it is his own. However, the mission is compromised by the thief's own troubled psyche. Christopher Nolan's sci-fi thriller, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, Elliot Page, Joseph Gordon-Levitt and Tom Hardy

programme.title: Jason Bourne (2016)
programme.desc: Former government operative Jason Bourne has been in hiding for 12 years after exposing a sinister CIA operation. However, he once again finds himself being hunted down when a former CIA operative gone rogue acquires top secret information - including details of Bourne's own father's involvement in a clandestine scheme. Action thriller, starring Matt Damon, Tommy Lee Jones, Alicia Vikander and Julia Stiles

programme.title: The Meg (2018)
programme.desc: A massive creature attacks a deep-sea submersible, leaving the crew of the disabled vessel trapped at the bottom of the Pacific. Rescue diver Jonas Taylor is hired to save the crew from a 75ft-long prehistoric shark, known as the Megalodon. Director Jon Turteltaub's action thriller, starring Jason Statham, Bingbing Li, Rainn Wilson and Ruby Rose

programme.title: 300: Rise of an Empire (2014)
programme.desc: Ruler of the Persian empire and self-proclaimed god Xerxes continues his campaign to crush the emerging Greek nation, forcing a general from the democratic city state of Athens to lead his navy into battle against Xerxes' vast armada. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Sullivan Stapleton, Eva Green and Lena Headey

programme.title: The Hurricane Heist (2018)
programme.desc: Thieves attempt a major heist against the US Treasury as a Category 5 hurricane approaches one of its mint facilities. Action thriller, starring Toby Kebbell, Maggie Grace and Ryan Kwanten.

programme.title: Transformers: Dark of the Moon (2011)
programme.desc: The heroic autobots discover that a spaceship from their own planet crashed into the moon decades ago, carrying their former leader and an incredibly powerful device. They must fight to defend the human race as their enemies the decepticons plot to use this machine to rebuild their own world and destroy the Earth. Sci-fi adventure sequel, with Shia LaBeouf, Josh Duhamel and Rosie Huntington-Whiteley

programme.title: Transformers: Age of Extinction (2014)
programme.desc: The shape-changing robots are driven into hiding when the human race turns against them. When rediscovered by a kindly inventor and his daughter, they come out of exile to save the world, battling a new breed of human-created Transformers. These deadly new machines were created to hunt them down, but are now growing independent and intend to make use of technology from the Transformers' home planet in a plot that could threaten all life on Earth. Fourth film in the sci-fi adventure series, starring Mark Wahlberg, Stanley Tucci and Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: 300: Rise of an Empire (2014)
programme.desc: Ruler of the Persian empire and self-proclaimed god Xerxes continues his campaign to crush the emerging Greek nation, forcing a general from the democratic city state of Athens to lead his navy into battle against Xerxes' vast armada. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Sullivan Stapleton, Eva Green and Lena Headey

programme.title: Inception (2010)
programme.desc: A talented thief uses hi-tech devices to enter other people's dreams so he can steal their secrets. An industrialist hires him to perform a far more challenging job - to implant an idea into a corporate heir's mind, so he will think it is his own. However, the mission is compromised by the thief's own troubled psyche. Christopher Nolan's sci-fi thriller, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, Elliot Page, Joseph Gordon-Levitt and Tom Hardy

programme.title: Jason Bourne (2016)
programme.desc: Former government operative Jason Bourne has been in hiding for 12 years after exposing a sinister CIA operation. However, he once again finds himself being hunted down when a former CIA operative gone rogue acquires top secret information - including details of Bourne's own father's involvement in a clandestine scheme. Action thriller, starring Matt Damon, Tommy Lee Jones, Alicia Vikander and Julia Stiles

programme.title: Jurassic World (2015)
programme.desc: Decades on from the creation of Jurassic Park, a fully operational attraction filled with genetically engineered dinosaurs has been opened on the island. In need of a new attraction, the company behind the park creates an entirely new species - only for it to escape and terrorise the tourists. Action adventure sequel, starring Chris Pratt and Bryce Dallas Howard

programme.title: Jurassic World: Fallen Kingdom (2018)
programme.desc: Three years after the destruction of Jurassic World, Owen Grady and Claire Dearing are asked to return to the theme park to save its resident dinosaurs from the island's active volcano. During their mission, they uncover a conspiracy that may affect the entire planet. Action sequel, starring Chris Pratt, Bryce Dallas Howard, Rafe Spall and Toby Jones

programme.title: Bumblebee (2018)
programme.desc: In 1987, lonely Californian teenager Charlie Watson is traumatised by the death of her father and resentful of her mother Sally's relationship with new boyfriend Ronald. For her 18th-birthday she's given a yellow Volkswagen Beetle, which turns out to be the battle-scarred Autobot Bumblebee. Transformers prequel, starring Hailee Steinfeld, John Cena and Jorge Lendeborg Jr

programme.title: Kong: Skull Island (2017)
programme.desc: Years after two enemy pilots land on a mysterious unchartered island in the Pacific, a team of scientists embark on a top-secret mission to the same region. Things soon descend into chaos when the local wildlife proves far from welcoming, and they must battle to escape from the domain of the mighty Kong. Action adventure, starring Tom Hiddleston, Samuel L Jackson and Brie Larson

programme.title: Transformers: Dark of the Moon (2011)
programme.desc: The heroic autobots discover that a spaceship from their own planet crashed into the moon decades ago, carrying their former leader and an incredibly powerful device. They must fight to defend the human race as their enemies the decepticons plot to use this machine to rebuild their own world and destroy the Earth. Sci-fi adventure sequel, with Shia LaBeouf, Josh Duhamel and Rosie Huntington-Whiteley

programme.title: Transformers: Age of Extinction (2014)
programme.desc: The shape-changing robots are driven into hiding when the human race turns against them. When rediscovered by a kindly inventor and his daughter, they come out of exile to save the world, battling a new breed of human-created Transformers. These deadly new machines were created to hunt them down, but are now growing independent and intend to make use of technology from the Transformers' home planet in a plot that could threaten all life on Earth. Fourth film in the sci-fi adventure series, starring Mark Wahlberg, Stanley Tucci and Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: The Shadow's Edge (2025)
programme.desc: A brilliant cyber-criminal gang evades the police's state-of-the-art surveillance system with a haul worth billions. Retired tracking expert Wong Tak-Chong is ushered back into active service to mentor rookie officer He Qiuguo and build an elite unit to stop the criminals. Action thriller, starring Jackie Chan, Zhang Zifeng and Tony Leung Ka-fai

programme.title: We Bury the Dead (2024)
programme.desc: When an experimental military weapon accidentally devastates Tasmania, volunteers travel to the island to help authorities identify the dead. Among them is a Ava Newman, who secretly hopes to find her missing husband. However, some of the bodies seem to be returning to life. Horror, starring Daisy Ridley, Brenton Thwaites and Mark Coles Smith

programme.title: The Cinema List: Race Against Time
programme.desc: This edition is about the race against time, from a deadly threat in Fuze to The Desperate Hour, where Naomi Watts faces every parent's nightmare - the clock is ticking

programme.title: Tafiti - Across The Desert (2025)
programme.desc: A young meerkat's grandpa is bitten by a snake, so he sets off to find a magical flower for the cure. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Cosima Henman, Steve Hudson and Kathleen Renish

programme.title: Spinal Tap II: The End Continues (2025)
programme.desc: Nigel Tufnel, David St Hubbins and Derek Smalls reunite after a 15-year break for one final concert. Their progress is charted once more by documentary maker Marti DiBergi, who offers perhaps too much perspective on their new direction. Comedy sequel, written by and starring Michael McKean, Harry Shearer, Christopher Guest and director Rob Reiner, in his final film. Kerry Godliman and Chris Addison also star with cameos by Paul McCartney, Elton John and Garth Brooks

programme.title: Gabby's Dollhouse: The Movie (2025)
programme.desc: When Gabby's prized dollhouse ends up with eccentric cat lady Vera, the youngster embarks on a adventure to reunite the Gabby Cats and retrieve her beloved diorama before it's too late. Comedy adventure, starring Laila Lockhart Kraner and Kristen Wiig

programme.title: Honey Don't (2025)
programme.desc: California private detective Honey O'Donahue delves into a series of unusual deaths connected to a mysterious church. Co-writer/director Ethan Coen's comedy mystery, starring Margaret Qualley, Aubrey Plaza and Chris Evans

programme.title: The Bride! (2026)
programme.desc: In 1930s Chicago, maverick scientist Dr Euphronius gives life to a murdered woman, sparking romance, police interest and radical change. Writer/director Maggie Gyllenhaal's horror based on The Bride of Frankenstein, starring Jessie Buckley, Christian Bale and Annette Bening

programme.title: Don't Let's Go to the Dogs Tonight (2025)
programme.desc: An eight-year-old girl experiences life on her family's Rhodesian farm during the final stages of the Bush War. Drama, written, directed by and starring Embeth Davidtz, with Lexi Ventner and Zikhona Bali

programme.title: Gabby's Dollhouse: The Movie (2025)
programme.desc: When Gabby's prized dollhouse ends up with eccentric cat lady Vera, the youngster embarks on a adventure to reunite the Gabby Cats and retrieve her beloved diorama before it's too late. Comedy adventure, starring Laila Lockhart Kraner and Kristen Wiig

programme.title: The Running Man (2025)
programme.desc: Desperate to save his sick daughter, Ben Richards enters a deadly televised competition where contestants must survive 30 days while being hunted by professional assassins. Director Edgar Wright's comedy thriller based on the Stephen King story, starring Glen Powell, Lee Pace and Josh Brolin

programme.title: Downton Abbey: The Grand Finale (2025)
programme.desc: In the summer of 1930, the Crawleys grapple with the threat of social disgrace when Mary finds herself at the centre of a public scandal. Romantic drama, starring Joely Richardson, Joanne Froggatt and Paul Giamatti

programme.title: Wuthering Heights (2026)
programme.desc: Catherine Earnshaw is raised by her alcoholic father at a Yorkshire farmhouse, with Nelly Dean as a constant companion. Mr Earnshaw 'acquires' an orphaned boy named Heathcliff during one of his gallivants and gifts the waif to Cathy as her pet. The children become inseparable, and as adults they embark on a destructive relationship. Writer-director Emerald Fennell's drama based on Emily Bronte's romance, starring Margot Robbie, Jacob Elordi and Martin Clunes

programme.title: The Bambers: Murder at the Farm
programme.desc: Bamber is imprisoned for life, but decides to challenge the verdict and clear his name. A band of campaigners and internet sleuths support him

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A profile of David Berkowitz, the serial killer who found himself at the centre of New York's biggest manhunt after a vicious shooting spree that left six dead

programme.title: Ireland's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Delving into Ireland's worst murder cases. In 2004, Joe O'Reilly murdered his wife and made it look like a burglary gone wrong. Before being caught, he even appealed for information on TV

programme.title: Murders That Shocked the Nation
programme.sub-title: Harold Jones - The Welsh Child Killer
programme.desc: The case of Harold Jones, found guilty of murder at the age of 15 in 1921, who is also suspected to have been 1960s serial killer 'Jack the Stripper'

programme.title: Britain's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A case highlighting the dangers of online grooming, in which 14-year-old Breck Bednar was lured to a London flat before being killed by a fellow teenager

programme.title: Send in the Dogs
programme.desc: PC Greaves and his four-year-old German shepherd Riley track down a motorist who panicked when he saw flashing blue lights and ran off, while PC Whitaker and his dog Roscoe have to disarm a man who has a 12-inch knife. During a search of three vehicles used by a suspected drug dealer, sniffer spaniel Smut and handler PC Carlin find a wallet with almost £1,000 inside it hidden under a dashboard. Narrated by Ken Stott

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness the best and worst behaviour on the nation's busiest motorway

programme.desc: Documentary following the work of New Zealand police officers as they try to keep motorists safe on some of the country's most dangerous roads

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: It's journey's end for a pair of jokers who think it's a jape to take a bus for a joyride

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood reveals how a gang of international bag thieves were brought down to earth by an aviation crime squad. Plus, thefts from an elderly woman - were her carers or her kin to blame?

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A shopkeeper tears the balaclava from his attacker, items mysteriously vanish from a locked house, and a villager reunites some rubbish with its rightful owner

programme.title: Caught on Dashcam
programme.desc: Fly-on-the-dashboard documentary showcasing outrageous road disasters, captured in video form by drivers' in-vehicle dashboard cameras

programme.title: Britain's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A fugitive from the United States, David Bieber, murders one British police officer and tries to kill two more, sparking a nationwide search

programme.title: Killer in My Village
programme.desc: The story of a young wife and mother-of-four, murdered ferociously by her controlling husband and an accomplice

programme.title: Forensics: Catching the Killer
programme.desc: When the body of a 16-year-old girl is found in a field, it would take over two decades before a DNA profile of the killer can be developed

programme.title: Death on the Beach
programme.desc: In 2015 Chrissie Annesley travelled to the paradise island of Koh Tao for the trip of a lifetime. Two weeks later she was found dead in her beach hut, the latest in a tragic roll call of young travellers to have died under mysterious circumstances on this most remote of Thailand's islands. This documentary asks whether Chrissie and the others were victims of misadventure or part of a series of violent murders

programme.title: How I Caught the Killer
programme.desc: How police used CCTV, eyewitness statements and a public appeal to find the killer of a 28-year-old woman, found dead on Nottingham's streets

programme.title: Murdered at First Sight
programme.desc: The tragic story of Debbie Buxton, who was walking her dogs at a local beauty spot when she was killed in a frenzied and unprovoked attack by David Bond

programme.title: Killer in My Village
programme.desc: The horrific case of Georgina Edmonds, a pensioner and grandmother who was bludgeoned to death by Matthew Hamlen

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: For Richard Kuklinski, crime was his way of life. As a professional murderer, his unusual methods led to a disturbing nickname, The Iceman

programme.title: Britain's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A look at Vincent Tabak, the man responsible for the murder of Joanna Yeates, which triggered one of the biggest-ever police investigations in Bristol

programme.title: Forensics: Catching the Killer
programme.desc: Experts analyse sand found in a car after a man goes missing, leading them to a body and a twisted murder case

programme.title: Killer in My Village
programme.desc: A look at the murder of 15-year-old Holly Newton, who was stabbed to death in an alleyway in the quiet town of Hexham

programme.title: The Bambers: Murder at the Farm
programme.desc: Jeremy's relationship with his parents comes under the microscope, revealing possible motives for the murders. The tabloids hunt his girlfriend, Julie

programme.title: The Real Manhunter
programme.desc: The story of Michael Danaher, who found himself deep in debt, prompting him to draw up a list of famous and wealthy robbery targets. But his plans resulted in vicious murder

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The lives and crimes of two cousins who, together, took the lives of 10 girls and women aged between 12 and 28

programme.title: Ireland's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: Britain's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: After a teenager's disappearance, a huge search campaign results in a truly depraved case striking at the very heart of a family

programme.title: Send in the Dogs
programme.desc: Father-and-son dog handlers John and Kevin Tozer join forces with their Alsatians Charlie and Floyd as the Metropolitan Police dog unit raids a suspected cannabis den. However, retirement beckons for John's canine companion, leaving 13-month-old Herbie as a possible replacement - if he can make it through basic training to join London's dog squad elite ranks. Narrated by Ken Stott

programme.desc: Callous crooks fleece an elderly couple in their hour of need, and locals bring back the village stocks when thieves steal from a roadside stall

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A criminal riding a motorbike is brought down by a policeman on two feet. A car thief is scared witless when he comes face to face with the vehicle's owner

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A jeweller robbed as his wife watched on her phone and the family who found a stranger in their house. Plus, How a policeman's intuition stopped an escaped convict in his tracks. Presented by Dom Littlewood

programme.desc: Fly-on-the-dashboard documentary showcasing outrageous road disasters, captured in video form by drivers' in-vehicle dashboard cameras

programme.title: Britain's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: Killer in My Village
programme.desc: The horrific case of Georgina Edmonds, a pensioner and grandmother who was bludgeoned to death by Matthew Hamlen

programme.title: Forensics: Catching the Killer
programme.desc: Thirty years after the gruesome murders of three women, familial DNA and the exhumation of a body finally closes the case

programme.title: Death on the Beach
programme.desc: After tracking down the last man to see his daughter alive, Boyne Annesley learns that shortly before her death Chrissie was asking questions about the double murders of Hannah Witheridge and David Miller on the island's Sairee Beach in 2014

programme.title: How I Caught the Killer
programme.desc: Detective Mike Southwell talks about his 1981 investigation into a missing persons case that contained one of the grimmest discoveries in British police history

programme.sub-title: Death at the Door: Ron Browning

programme.desc: The story of artist and grandmother Valerie Graves, who was killed in a horrific attack just days after Christmas while housesitting a million-pound property

programme.title: Digging Up Britain's Past
programme.sub-title: Invincible - The Royal Navy's Greatest Ship
programme.desc: Raksha Dave and Alex Langlands examine the history of HMS Invincible, which was captured from the French in 1747 and helped transform the Royal Navy with the lessons it taught about organisation and ship design. For more than 260 years, HMS Invincible lay at the bottom of the Solent, but archaeologists are now diving beneath the waves in a £2m excavation to discover more about this game-changing ship

programme.title: Secrets in the Ice
programme.desc: Bizarre ice formations in the Antarctic Ocean are investigated and a discovery off the east coast of Canada may be connected to a tragic event. In the most remote, cold, and inhospitable places on the planet, scientists and adventurers have uncovered mysteries that have been frozen in time

programme.title: Canadian Pickers
programme.desc: The skilled 'pickers' strike gold in Quebec, where they find an unusual Inuit carving and heaps of antique farming equipment. Plus, Scott puts in an outrageous offer for a rare tin can

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: In a feature-length episode, theorist Giorgio Tsoukalos explores the latest scientific discoveries at some of the world's most ancient places to find evidence that extraterrestrials visited Egypt in the distant past

programme.title: History of Weapons
programme.desc: Military historian Stephen Bull tests the three most significant weapons of the sea and explores whether the subsonic torpedo could rule the waves in the future

programme.desc: The story of Adolf Hitler's so-called 'miracle' weapons - the V1 cruise missile and the V2 ballistic missile. These long-range artillery weapons posed a significant threat to the Allies

programme.title: WWII Battles in Colour
programme.desc: The most important battles of the European theatre. Rare archive, colourised for the first time, charts the German Army in 1939 as it unleashes a new Blitzkrieg - lightning war - tactic. This documentary features radio broadcasts, oral histories, memoirs and personal diaries from both sides of the battle lines. Supporting the words of the combatants is expert analyses by military and social historians

programme.title: WWII Battles in Colour
programme.desc: Gripping real life stories - when the fate of nations hung in the balance - are brought to life in never-before-seen colourised battle footage. This instalment illustrates life and death on the battlefield of El Alamein and life behind the lines in the north African desert

programme.title: WWII Battles in Colour
programme.desc: The Battle of Kursk, a Second World War engagement between German and Soviet forces on the Eastern Front near Kursk, roughly 280 miles south west of Moscow, during July and August 1943. This archive footage, colourised for the first time, tells the story of the greatest tank battle the world has ever seen

programme.title: WWII Battles in Colour
programme.desc: The Battle of the Bulge, a major German offensive campaign on the Western Front during the Second World War, which took place in December 1944 and January 1945. Film, colorised for the first time, brings eye-witness accounts from those who fought in the US military's bloodiest battle on the European front in the conflict

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There: Military Mysteries
programme.sub-title: Jungle Horrors, Project Giant, Homesick Mechanic, Inside Narco Subs
programme.desc: Efforts by the US military during the Vietnam War to convince the enemy it was under attack by evil spirits. Plus, the theft of one of the most powerful military planes in the world by a man who had never flown a plane and the facts behind a giant underground base built by the Nazis during the Second World War

programme.title: Greatest Escapes of WWII
programme.desc: Some of the most incredible escapes of the conflict, including a daring dash through Nazi Europe, a deadly mountain escape and the Second World War's largest breakout, by men who chose death over dishonour

programme.title: Greatest Escapes of WWII
programme.desc: Some of the most incredible escapes of the conflict, including a daring dash through Nazi Europe, a deadly mountain escape and the Second World War's largest breakout, by men who chose death over dishonour

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: Veteran actor William Shatner examines the possibility of contact with otherworldly intelligent life, and how man should react to and handle such a situation. Plus, the practicality of man travelling beyond the moon

programme.title: Secrets in the Dark
programme.desc: Documentary shedding light on unusual discoveries made in the darkest places on Earth and venturing into the realm of the supernatural, where eerie and unsettling findings turn up unexplained phenomena that challenge man's understanding of the world. This episode visits the ancient Burnt City of Shahre Sukhteh, Iran, as archaeologists uncover a remarkable discovery - the oldest-known prosthetic eye, made of gold

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: In a feature-length episode, theorist Giorgio Tsoukalos explores the latest scientific discoveries at some of the world's most ancient places to find evidence that extraterrestrials visited Egypt in the distant past

programme.title: UFO Hunters
programme.desc: The team examines a declassified US Department of Defence report which analyses an alleged encounter between a fighter pilot and a UFO in May 1980

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: An investigation into the last pitched battle to have taken place on British soil, the Battle of Culloden in 1746, on the outskirts of Inverness. Rick Edwards and river detectorist Beau Ouimette search for clues as to where Bonnie Prince Charlie, crossed the River Nairn as he fled the battlefield pursued by government forces. The battle was to have an impact for decades to follow on those on the losing side, the Jacobites who wanted to restore the Stuart lineage to the British throne. The Jacobite forces were shot down as they carried out their famous Highland Charge across the moorland, and some of those who survived escaped and crossed the River Nairn in an attempt to return to the relative safety of their homelands

programme.title: Digging Up Britain's Past
programme.desc: Archaeologists conduct a dig at Auckland Castle in Co Durham, which 700 years ago was home to a group of power-crazed bishops who behaved more like warriors than religious leaders. The experts head to Dirleton Castle in East Lothian to reveal the role one of the men played in defending England from William Wallace's army, and also try their hands at the art of stained glass-making, and fire a working trebuchet - the ultimate medieval weapon of war

programme.title: Secrets in the Ice
programme.desc: Mysterious industrial waste and abandoned homes are discovered in a remote, uninhabited part of Greenland. In Norway, strange ditches and huge mounds are found deep within the Arctic Circle. A huge pyramid rivalling those of ancient Egypt is found far out on the Kazakh steppe. Plus, a perfectly shaped ball of golden fur melts out of the frozen ground of the Yukon's gold fields

programme.title: Canadian Pickers
programme.desc: Scott and Sheldon land in Quebec's Habs country and dig into Canada's longest-running bar and pull out nine tables, reportedly from the tavern owned by Henri 'Pocket Rocket' Richard, the famed hockey player

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The advancement of artificial intelligence and whether it could lead to human extinction or if humans could merge with AI to become a cyborg

programme.title: History of Weapons
programme.desc: Experts explore how the ambush has revolutionised warfare with the benefit of undetected weapons, from the caltrop to the insidious land mine

programme.title: Greatest Escapes of WWII
programme.desc: A Russian soldier's daring escape from a Nazi death camp, a bold plan from a Japanese prison and pilots winning their enemies' respect

programme.title: Bomber: Terror of WWII
programme.desc: After the end of the Battle of Britain came the bombing of German cities. In the war in the Pacific, the US and Japan successfully bombed naval and land targets

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The advancement of artificial intelligence and whether it could lead to human extinction or if humans could merge with AI to become a cyborg

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Experts investigate the similarities in burial tombs from distant cultures, and question whether aliens could have influenced their complicated designs

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Investigations into whether prophets such as Elijah and Joseph Smith communicated with a heavenly source, or came into contact with extraterrestrials

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The hundreds of gigantic lines, some in the shape of animals, geometric patterns and even an alien-like being, that extend for several miles throughout a remote Peruvian desert. Known as the Nazca Lines, some theorists believe the markings have an extraterrestrial connection, and that the ancient people of the region possibly created these figures in tribute to the gods who visited them

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The story of Travis Walton, who claims that in 1975 he was taken aboard a space ship inhabited by extraterrestrial beings. Plus, an analysis of the the Hebrew Bible, which is filled with stories of abductions by otherworldly beings

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: An analysis of ancient monuments, questioning what compelled man to move colossal stones long distances to assemble remarkably similar configurations in several locations around the world. Some people believe that extraterrestrials instructed these complex structures be built. Presented by Giorgio Tsoukalos

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There: Military Mysteries
programme.sub-title: Curious Case of Major Logan, The Lost Cosmonaut, Battleship Explosion, Dolphins As Secret Weapons
programme.desc: Wartime mysteries and alleged cover-ups, including the case of an American pilot shot down during the Korean War and declared dead. The programme investigates whether he was really captured and taken prisoner by the Soviets. Plus, the strange recording that may have captured the real first woman in space dying on re-entry and a US training programme said to be employing dolphins to kill

programme.title: Greatest Escapes of WWII
programme.desc: A look at some of the most incredible escapes of the conflict, featuring a German breakout, a US bomber crew's Soviet secret and the Trojan Horse of Stalag Luft III

programme.title: Greatest Escapes of WWII
programme.desc: A look at some of the most incredible escapes of the conflict, featuring a German breakout, a US bomber crew's Soviet secret and the Trojan Horse of Stalag Luft III

programme.title: History's Greatest Myths
programme.desc: The influence that popular culture and entertainment have on daily life. As people gravitate to larger-than-life stories, outlandish myths can begin to spread

programme.title: Egypt's Unexplained Files
programme.desc: Scientists and historians join forces to investigate whether colossal volcanoes toppled an ancient dynasty, and reveal the true face of an ancient mummy

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The advancement of artificial intelligence and whether it could lead to human extinction or if humans could merge with AI to become a cyborg

programme.title: UFO Hunters
programme.desc: The Hudson Valley area of upstate New York, where more than 7,000 UFO sightings have been reported. This edition speculates whether the area is home to one of the many extra-terrestrial gateways that are believed to be spread all over the world

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: Rick Edwards and Beau Ouimette head to north Wales to investigate waterways that may contain signs of the medieval Welsh Rebellion. Conwy Castle was constructed by Edward I between 1283-1287 after the Welsh Rebellion of 1282-3. The castle was part of a chain of fortifications known as the 'Iron Ring of Castles' and was both a material and symbolic sign of English power and dominance. Edward I selected its site on a high rock perched above the estuary of the River Conwy so that the castle could be supplied by the river in times of siege. Day two finds the pair at Ruthin Castle, a medieval fortress overlooking the River Clwyd

programme.title: Digging Up Britain's Past
programme.desc: Centuries ago, canals were the liquid highways of Britain, transporting raw materials and finished goods all over the country. Their arrival kick-started the Industrial Revolution, which was to turn Britain into a superpower. The very first water superhighway was the Bridgewater Canal, completed in 1761 to carry coal to Manchester from the mines in Worsley. Here, a team of archaeologists from Salford University roots out this industrial past, working with volunteers to excavate the very coal mine that inspired the creation of the canal

programme.title: Secrets in the Ice
programme.desc: A Kazakh farmer discovers ancient burial sites that may determine whether or not the legend of the Amazon women warriors is myth or reality. In the Italian Alps, a group of hikers explores long forgotten military infrastructure that points to the region's complicated and violent history. A shrine in the Indian Himalayas has long been said to be protected by supernatural powers, but strange markings on the temple reveal it was subject to an almost equally as powerful force. Palaeontologists discover a fossilised species unknown to science embedded in a rock hundreds of millions of years old

programme.title: Canadian Pickers
programme.desc: The pickers hit the city of Niagara Falls, Ontario, on their hunt for big deals. Their first stop finds them in the middle of the strip staring down a collector's memorabilia and bizarre pieces. They later head to view items at the city's oldest restaurant

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The push for private commercial space flight and the launch of Donald Trump's Space Force. The programme includes a discussion of the scientific effort to look for traces of Martian life, the idea that Earth life originated from Martian life that travelled via a space rock and a look at the challenges likely to be faced if attempts are made to colonise the moon and Mars

programme.title: Hell on Earth: WWII
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the story of the Second World War. The first episode retraces the beginning of the German invasion of Poland and the segregation of Jewish people

programme.title: World War II with Tom Hanks
programme.desc: The actor explores the key events of the Second World War, from Germany's invasion of Poland through the atomic age, examining pivotal battles, wartime leaders like Churchill and Roosevelt, and the human cost of total war. programme.title: World War II with Tom Hanks
